data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Adobe.com utilizes a microservices architecture to allow independent deployment and scaling of different parts of the application.
data.technical_spec: Each service operates in its own process and communicates through a well-defined API.

data.title: Cloud Hosting with AWS
data.description: The majority of Adobe's services are hosted on Amazon Web Services (AWS), providing scalability and reliability.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes EC2, S3, and RDS among other services.

data.title: Kubernetes for Orchestration
data.description: Adobe uses Kubernetes to manage and automate the deployment, scaling, and operations of application containers.

data.title: Content Delivery Network Integration
data.description: Adobe implements a CDN to ensure fast loading times globally.
data.technical_spec: Integration with Akamai for global content distribution.

data.title: API-First Design
data.description: Adobe's services are built with an API-first approach to integrate various products.
data.technical_spec: RESTful and GraphQL APIs with robust documentation.
